key_insight: EVMDOT turns dynamic optimal transport into an unconstrained energetic variational formulation, enabling direct reconstruction of energy landscapes and velocity fields from time-series density data.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical way to infer latent system dynamics and transition pathways from noisy observational data in physics-inspired, biological, and medical applications.
method: Uses the flow map to enforce the continuity equation automatically.
Combines least action with maximum dissipation to recover the velocity field.
Applied to the Fokker--Planck equation to estimate energy and Waddington landscapes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing robust reconstruction under data quantity--quality trade-offs and an application to ADNI data that identifies two disease-related landscape wells and a transition path.
watch_item: Landscape inference depends on the availability of sufficient time-series density data, so sparse or highly biased observations may still limit reliability.
paper_title: Learning the Energy Landscapes of Dynamical Systems via Energetic Variational Optimal Transport under Data Quantity--Quality Trade-offs
